blockingIssues.description: src/resources/fine_tuning.rs omits the supported `label_model` grader variant, so label-model graders cannot be sent and validate responses containing `type: "label_model"` will not deserialize.

The feature claims VAL-ADV-001 coverage, but only adds `tests/live_fine_tuning_graders_smoke.rs`; there is no live fine-tuning job smoke proving the required budget-capped create/retrieve/list/cancel lifecycle against the real API.

src/resources/evals.rs models eval-run statuses as `running` / `succeeded` / `cancelled`, but the documented surface uses `in_progress` / `completed` / `canceled`, so real eval-run payloads lose typed status semantics.
The feature never added the contract-required live eval-run/output-item smoke, so VAL-ADV-006 and VAL-ADV-007 lack the required real-API proof for run cancel semantics and output-item inspection.

The container read models/tests currently claim round-tripping of `file_ids`, `skills`, and allowlist `domain_secrets`, but the current public response models/examples do not document those request-only fields on retrieve/list responses, so the asserted read contract is not convincingly proven.

`Videos::create` falls back to JSON for non-upload references even though the documented `/videos` create route uses multipart form-data regardless of whether the input reference is an upload or nested asset object.

`Videos::edit` and `Videos::extend` take JSON paths for `VideoSource::Id`, but the documented `/videos/edits` and `/videos/extensions` routes also use multipart form-data for existing-video-id transforms.

appliedUpdates.description: Added an advanced-platform note to `.factory/library/api-surface.md` that current public Containers read models/examples do not document request-only `file_ids`, `skills`, or allowlist `domain_secrets` on retrieve/list responses.

Added an advanced-platform note to `.factory/library/api-surface.md` that Videos `create`, `edit`, and `extend` remain multipart form-data endpoints even when using existing asset/video references rather than fresh uploads.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require feature manifests and worker verification plans to include every live-proof command named by any fulfilled validation-contract assertion, especially when a feature claims coverage for live-smoke assertions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `fine-tuning-jobs-and-graders` and `evals-and-eval-runs` claimed fulfilled assertions whose validation-contract entries require live run/job smokes, but their `features.json` verification steps only listed narrower live checks (`live_fine_tuning_graders_smoke` and `live_evals_crud_smoke`). That plan drift let missing live proof ship into scrutiny.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that for entitlement-gated or expensive advanced-platform APIs, low-cost live validation may use list/retrieve style entitlement probes when they still prove the required contract, and the handoff should explicitly justify that probe choice.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `skills-and-videos-surfaces` handoff explicitly requested this clarification after using skip-friendly entitlement-aware live behavior, and the current skill text only says to run a low-cost live smoke without naming acceptable probe strategies for expensive job surfaces.

rejectedObservations.observation: The evals review repeated the same feature-manifest-versus-validation-contract drift problem already observed in the fine-tuning review.
